title: Volvo just made public EV charging a lot less annoying
summary: Volvo EX90 drivers in the US can now charge at public charging networks, including Superchargers and IONNA DC fast chargers, without opening an app or pulling out a credit card.

title: Tesla HW3 claim grows to 7,000 owners, gets law firm backing for collective action
summary:
The Dutch collective claim against Tesla over broken “Full Self-Driving” promises on Hardware 3 vehicles has reached nearly 7,000 verified participants and secured backing from law firm Kennedy Van der Laan for a formal legal action.

The initiative, which launched in April with 3,000 signups in its first week, is now preparing to transfer the case to a foundation that will pursue the claim on behalf of all participants under Dutch collective action law.

title: Subaru announces 2027 Solterra EV prices remain under $40,000 after a major upgrade
summary: After a complete overhaul in 2026, the 2027 Subaru Solterra will go on sale at the same price, starting at under $40,000.

title: California solar is crushing natural gas this year
summary: California’s utility-scale solar plants are generating more electricity than natural gas for most days of the year in 2026, according to new data from the US Energy Information Administration (EIA).

title: BMW opens i3 orders months ahead of schedule as demand takes off
summary: The new i3 was set to go on sale this fall, but BMW opened orders on Thursday, months ahead of schedule, due to strong demand.

title: Honda and QuantumScape (QS) team up to make ‘game-changing’ solid-state batteries
summary: Honda and QuantumScape (QS) announced a new partnership to commercialize solid-state batteries for EVs and more.

title: Heybike sale up to $1,000 e-bike savings from $999, Anker SOLIX 24-hour flash sale on 508Wh to 7,680Wh power stations, Hiboy, more
summary:
We’ve got a mix of our top four Green Deal categories included in this week’s Thursday edition, starting with Heybike’s Father’s Day Sale that is lasting through Prime Day week with up to $1,000 e-bike savings, like the Hero Carbon Fiber All-Terrain e-bikes (rear or mid-drive motors) at their lows from $1,899, among others. We also have a 24-hour Anker SOLIX flash sale with exclusive bonus savings on 508Wh to 7,680Wh power station units starting from $265, as well as Hiboy’s Father’s Day Sale lineup with up to 60% discounts, like the U2 Pro Fat Tire Cargo Electric Scooter at its $900 low, the EGO Power+ 15-inch Multi-Head String Trimmer kit at the best price since October, and more waiting for you below. Don’t forget about all the hangover deals either, which have been collected together at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Velotric Prime Day e-bike sale lineup with up to $862 savings, the latest EcoFlow flash sale offers with another day left, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Sweden tells EU to reject Tesla ‘FSD’ unless speeding is removed
summary:
Sweden’s transport authority has told the European Union it should vote against approving Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving (Supervised)” across the bloc unless the system’s ability to exceed posted speed limits is removed.

The recommendation, in a previously unreported April 30 letter to the EU’s Technical Committee on Motor Vehicles (TCMV), lands just before the committee is due to take up the matter on June 30, ahead of an eventual vote on a bloc-wide rollout.

title: The Ford Mustang Mach-e is on borrowed time as more affordable EVs roll in
summary: After discontinuing the all-electric F-150 Lightning, it looks like the Mustang Mach-E is also running out of time. Ford is preparing to launch a new generation of EVs that are more affordable, more efficient, and more profitable, starting with the $30,000 pickup in 2027.

title: Tesla teases new FSD features — but it’s still not the self-driving promised
summary:
Elon Musk says Tesla’s Full Self-Driving will soon remember your parking preferences and let you talk to it through Grok, like giving directions to an Uber driver.

The features sound useful, and they reflect how genuinely impressive FSD has become — but they also underscore that it remains a supervised driver-assist system, not the unsupervised self-driving Tesla has sold to owners for years.

title: Skipping The Ferrari Luce Could Cost Collectors The Cars They Really Want
summary: Ferrari’s first EV may be controversial, but for loyal collectors, saying no could make the next dream allocation more difficult.

title: The 2027 Porsche Taycan Gets Fake Gear Shifts And A Bigger Standard Battery
summary: The refreshed Taycan has a bigger standard battery and more theater through simulated gears.

title: Uber, Stellantis, And Wayve Want To Blanket The World In Robotaxis
summary: Uber seems to have spent the past year attaching itself to every major robotaxi effort it can, and now Stellantis is joining that list.

title: Tesla Owners Are Using Doll Heads To Fool Full Self-Driving
summary: Is that Dwayne Johnson driving your Tesla? Nope, just his likeness being used to defeat Tesla's driver monitoring systems.

title: BMW Is Opening i3 Orders Way Ahead Of Schedule Due To Huge Demand
summary: BMW had originally planned to open the order books for the all-electric 3 Series this fall. Customers had other ideas.

title: Rivian Lays Off Hundreds Of Workers A Week After R2 Launch
summary: Rivian cut employees from its service and customer division on Tuesday, the The Wall Street Journal reported.

title: The Audi A6 Allroad Is Now A Plug-In Hybrid Antidote To Boring SUVs
summary: Thanks to a big battery pack, the new A6 Allroad wagon can deliver nearly 60 miles of electric range on the WLTP cycle.

title: Telo's Tiny Electric Truck Moves Closer To Reality With An Important New Partner
summary: Telo's adorable truck just hit a major milestone as the company finds a manufacturing partner for the EV's body structure.

title: Full Tesla Cybercab Specs: EPA Documents Confirm Battery Size, Weight, Power, And Preliminary Range
summary: The first front-wheel-drive Tesla will weigh way less than a Model 3 but still offer plenty of range.

title: A Chinese Car Brand Says It Hired A Top Ferrari Designer. Ferrari Wants A Name
summary: Luxeed says it hired a former Ferrari chief designer. Ferrari’s China PR boss publicly challenged the claim by asking who that was.
